content: I will apply the new highlight color (`highlight-500` / `highlight-50` / `highlight-700`) to the sidebar gradient, metrics card, and AI Match sparkles in `CompanyDashboard.tsx`.
thinking:
**Adjusting the Gradient**

Okay, I've just updated the sidebar gradient! It now transitions from `primary-600` and `primary-700` to `highlight-700` rather than `indigo-800`. This should achieve the desired blue aesthetic.

args.Description: "Apply the #533af6 highlight color to the sidebar gradient, Candidate stats card, and the AI Match sparkles."
args.Instruction: "Modify CompanyDashboard.tsx to use the new highlight color instead of generic indigo in specific locations."
args.ReplacementChunks: [{"AllowMultiple":false,"EndLine":1309,"ReplacementContent":"      <aside className={`w-64 bg-gradient-to-b from-primary-600 via-primary-700 to-highlight-700 p-6 flex flex-col fixed h-full z-50 shadow-2xl transition-transform duration-300 ${","StartLine":1306,"TargetContent":"      <aside className={`w-64 bg-gradient-to-b from-primary-600 via-primary-700 to-indigo-800 p-6 flex flex-col fixed h-full z-50 shadow-2xl transition-transform duration-300 ${"},{"AllowMultiple":false,"EndLine":1418,"ReplacementContent":"                    { label: 'Candidatos', value: totalCandidatesCount.toString(), trend: 'Inscritos', trendUp: true, icon: Users, color: 'text-highlight-500', bg: 'bg-highlight-50' },","StartLine":1416,"TargetContent":"                    { label: 'Candidatos', value: totalCandidatesCount.toString(), trend: 'Inscritos', trendUp: true, icon: Users, color: 'text-indigo-600', bg: 'bg-indigo-50' },"},{"AllowMultiple":false,"EndLine":2755,"ReplacementContent":"                                          <Sparkles size={11} className=\"text-highlight-500 animate-pulse shrink-0\" /> Match IA","StartLine":2752,"TargetContent":"                                          <Sparkles size={11} className=\"text-indigo-500 animate-pulse shrink-0\" /> Match IA"}]
